meta_title: Roofing in Pembroke Park, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated roofing in Pembroke Park, FL. CertainTeed shingles & West Lake tile. Licensed, insured, A+ BBB. Get a free estimate today!
h1: Trusted Roofing Replacement & Repair in Pembroke Park, Florida
intro:
Pembroke Park may be one of Broward County's smaller municipalities, but the rooftops over its homes face the same punishing South Florida weather as anywhere else in the region. Nestled between Hollywood to the east and West Park to the south, this close-knit community is home to hardworking families who have invested significantly in their properties — properties that average around $270,000 in value and deserve real protection. From Pembroke Park Estates to the neighborhoods surrounding the C.B. Smith Park area, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is proud to serve as the roofing contractor this community can count on season after season.

With approximately 55% of Pembroke Park's housing stock built before 1994, a large portion of local homes were constructed before Florida's modern building codes transformed the standards for roofing systems. These older structures — many featuring original shingle or tile roofs that have weathered decades of tropical sun, humidity, and at least nine named storms since 1990 — are statistically overdue for a professional inspection or full replacement. Even roofs that appear intact to an untrained eye may be harboring hidden vulnerabilities: cracked or missing tiles, deteriorated underlayment, lifted shingles, and compromised flashing that can lead to catastrophic interior water damage during a hurricane or even a typical afternoon summer storm.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we specialize in full roof replacements and targeted repairs using hurricane-rated roofing systems that are engineered specifically for South Florida's demanding climate. Our team brings 60+ years of combined experience to every project in Pembroke Park, and with 500+ homes completed across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, we understand the specific code requirements, permit processes, and weather conditions that apply right here in ZIP codes 33009 and 33023. Whether your roof is showing signs of wear or you're proactively upgrading before hurricane season, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is your local partner for lasting, code-compliant roofing solutions.

section_why.h2: Why Pembroke Park Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Roofing
section_why.body:
When it comes to replacing or repairing a roof in Pembroke Park, the choice of contractor matters as much as the choice of materials. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is not a middleman operation that subcontracts your project to an unfamiliar crew. Every single installation we perform is done entirely in-house, by our own trained, licensed technicians who show up to your home ready to deliver the same level of craftsmanship that has earned us an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau and hundreds of five-star reviews from homeowners across Broward County. When we say we're 'The Kings of Service,' that's not just a tagline — it's a standard we hold ourselves to on every job site, every day.

Our experience working in communities like Pembroke Park means we understand the nuances of local roofing. Older concrete block homes in Pembroke Park Estates often have unique deck configurations and truss systems that require careful evaluation before any new roofing system is installed. Our estimators don't just measure square footage — they evaluate the entire structure, assess the condition of the existing decking, and provide honest recommendations about what your home truly needs. We never upsell unnecessary services, and we never cut corners to pad profit margins.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is fully licensed and insured to operate in Broward County, and we handle the entire permitting process through the Town of Pembroke Park Building Department on your behalf. From pulling the initial permit to scheduling final inspections, we manage every administrative step so you don't have to. This end-to-end accountability is part of what sets us apart from fly-by-night contractors who disappear after storm season. We're a permanent, established presence in South Florida, and we'll be here for you long after your new roof is installed.

For homeowners managing tight budgets in a lower-middle income community like Pembroke Park, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing also offers flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down. A new roof is one of the most important investments you can make in your home, and we believe every homeowner deserves access to quality roofing regardless of their immediate financial situation. Our financing partners work with a wide range of credit profiles to find solutions that fit your household.

section_history.h2: Storm History That Proves Pembroke Park Roofs Must Be Built to Last
section_history.body:
Pembroke Park's location in central Broward County — approximately five miles from the Atlantic coastline — places it squarely in the path of the same powerful weather systems that have hammered South Florida for decades. The community has lived through at least nine named storms since 1990, and three in particular left lasting marks on the local housing stock: Hurricane Andrew in 1992, Hurricane Wilma in 2005, and Hurricane Irma in 2017. Each of these events exposed critical weaknesses in roofing systems that were either outdated, improperly installed, or simply not built to withstand sustained 170 mph wind zone conditions.

Hurricane Andrew, the benchmark catastrophe that reshaped Florida's entire building code infrastructure, devastated roofing systems across Broward and Miami-Dade counties with sustained winds exceeding 165 mph at its peak. Many of the homes that suffered the worst damage in communities like Pembroke Park had roofs installed before modern nailing patterns, enhanced underlayment requirements, and deck attachment standards were enforced. Hurricane Wilma in 2005 then delivered a one-two punch to roofs that had survived Andrew but were nearing the end of their serviceable life, and Irma in 2017 further stressed roofing systems with prolonged exposure to tropical storm-force winds over an extended period.

The lesson embedded in this storm history is clear: in Pembroke Park, a roof is not a passive structure. It is your home's first and most critical line of defense against wind, rain, and storm surge-driven debris. If your home was built before 1994 and has never received a comprehensive roofing upgrade, the risk of catastrophic failure during the next major storm is very real. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's hurricane-rated roofing systems are designed to change that equation, giving Pembroke Park families the peace of mind that comes from knowing their home can stand up to whatever the Atlantic hurricane season delivers.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Roofing in Pembroke Park
section_code.body:
While Pembroke Park is not classified as part of Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation that applies specifically to Miami-Dade and Broward coastal communities with the most extreme wind exposure — the town still falls under Florida Building Code requirements that mandate hurricane-rated roofing products for all new installations and replacements. Broward County sits within a 170 mph wind design zone, which means every roofing system installed must be tested, rated, and approved to perform under those conditions. Non-compliant materials simply cannot be permitted or legally installed.

For shingle roofing in Pembroke Park, the Florida Building Code requires a minimum 6-nail fastening pattern using ring-shank nails, a self-adhering modified bitumen underlayment that serves as a secondary water barrier, and products that carry a Florida Product Approval number demonstrating they've passed the required wind resistance testing. Tile roofing systems must meet similar standards, with specific requirements for mortar or mechanical attachment depending on roof pitch and exposure. These requirements exist because research following Hurricane Andrew proved definitively that proper underlayment and fastening systems are the difference between a roof that survives a hurricane and one that becomes airborne debris.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we handle the permit process through the Town of Pembroke Park Building Department from start to finish. We prepare all documentation, submit product approval numbers, schedule the required inspections, and ensure every phase of your roofing installation meets or exceeds code requirements. Upon project completion, you receive a properly closed permit — a critical document when it comes time to sell your home, file an insurance claim, or refinance your mortgage. Working with an unlicensed or unpermitted contractor might seem like a cost-saving shortcut, but it exposes you to significant legal and financial liability that no homeowner should risk.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed Shingles and West Lake Tile: Premium Roofing Systems Built for Pembroke Park
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two industry-leading roofing product lines that are ideally matched to the conditions and aesthetic preferences found in Pembroke Park's residential neighborhoods: CertainTeed architectural shingles from the Landmark series and West Lake concrete and clay tile systems. Both product lines carry Florida Product Approvals, are rated for the 170 mph wind zone requirements applicable to Pembroke Park, and come backed by manufacturer warranties that protect your investment for decades.

CertainTeed's Landmark series is one of the most trusted architectural shingle lines in North America, and for good reason. These dimensional, laminated shingles offer the visual depth and character of wood shake without the maintenance burden, and they're engineered to resist wind uplift, impact, and algae growth — all concerns that are particularly relevant in South Florida's hot, humid environment. The Landmark series carries a Class A fire rating and is available in a range of colors that complement both older-construction block homes and newer construction throughout Pembroke Park. For budget-conscious homeowners who want a durable, high-performance roof without the premium price tag of tile, CertainTeed Landmark shingles represent an exceptional value.

For homeowners who prefer the timeless elegance and superior longevity of tile roofing, West Lake's concrete and clay tile systems deliver unmatched durability and curb appeal. West Lake tiles are manufactured to withstand the rigors of South Florida's climate, offering excellent thermal performance that can actually reduce cooling loads in your home — a meaningful benefit given the region's year-round heat. Concrete tile systems are heavier and require proper structural evaluation, while clay tile offers a lighter alternative with a beautiful, traditional aesthetic. Both options are mechanically attached or mortar-set according to Florida Building Code specifications, ensuring that your new tile roof performs as beautifully in a Category 3 hurricane as it does on a calm Pembroke Park afternoon.

section_cost.h2: What Does a New Roof Cost in Pembroke Park — and How Do You Pay for It?
section_cost.body:
For homeowners in Pembroke Park, where average home values hover around $270,000, a roof replacement represents a significant but essential investment. The typical cost of a full roof replacement in the Pembroke Park market ranges from $10,000 to $22,000 depending on the size of the home, the slope and complexity of the roof, the product selected, and the condition of the existing decking. Architectural shingle systems like CertainTeed Landmark generally fall on the lower end of that range, while West Lake concrete or clay tile installations — which require more labor-intensive preparation and attachment — tend to command a higher investment reflective of their superior longevity and curb appeal.

The return on that investment is well documented. Real estate data consistently shows that a new roof increases resale value by an average of 60-70% of its cost, and in a market like Pembroke Park where buyers are highly attuned to roof age due to insurance requirements, a freshly installed, permitted roof can be the deciding factor in a sale. Beyond resale value, a new hurricane-rated roof reduces the risk of catastrophic interior damage during storm season — damage that can cost tens of thousands of dollars in structural repairs, mold remediation, and personal property loss.

Because Premier Impact Windows & Roofing understands that budget constraints are a reality for many Pembroke Park families, we offer flexible financing solutions with payments starting at just $87 per month and $0 down options available for qualified homeowners. Our financing partners work with a broad range of credit profiles, and our team will walk you through every available option during your free estimate. When you factor in the insurance savings that a new wind-rated roof can generate — which we'll cover in detail below — many homeowners find that a new roof effectively pays for itself over time through reduced premiums and avoided claim costs.

section_insurance.h2: How a New Roof in Pembroke Park Can Dramatically Reduce Your Homeowners Insurance Premiums
section_insurance.body:
Florida's homeowners insurance market has become increasingly volatile in recent years, with carriers tightening underwriting standards, raising premiums, and in some cases refusing to renew policies on homes with aging roofs. In Broward County communities like Pembroke Park, many homeowners have received non-renewal notices specifically citing roof age as the disqualifying factor. A new hurricane-rated roof installation doesn't just protect your home from storm damage — it can be the key to maintaining insurable status and accessing significantly lower premium rates.

Florida's wind mitigation inspection program allows licensed inspectors to evaluate the storm-resistance features of your roof and provide a formal report to your insurance carrier. When your new roof meets the criteria for features like a hip roof shape, enhanced deck attachment (using ring-shank nails in a 6d or 8d pattern), a secondary water resistance barrier (the self-adhering underlayment), and opening protections, those credits can translate into wind mitigation discounts of 20% to 45% off your annual wind insurance premium. For a Pembroke Park homeowner paying $3,500 to $5,000 annually in homeowners insurance — which is common in today's market — that represents real, recurring savings of $700 to $2,250 every year.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's installations are specifically designed and documented to maximize your wind mitigation credits. We use ring-shank nails, apply code-required secondary water barriers, and ensure proper roof deck attachment throughout — not because it's convenient, but because it's the right way to build a roof in South Florida. After your new roof is installed and the permit is finalized, we recommend scheduling a wind mitigation inspection immediately so you can present the updated report to your insurance carrier and begin capturing your savings at the earliest possible renewal. Our team is happy to connect you with trusted local inspectors and walk you through the process of submitting your updated mitigation report.

faqs.q: How do I know if my Pembroke Park home needs a full roof replacement or just repairs?
faqs.a: The decision depends on the age of your roof, the extent of the damage, and the condition of your decking and underlayment. In Pembroke Park, where 55% of homes were built before 1994, many roofs are operating well beyond their intended service life. A professional inspection by Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's team will evaluate all layers of your roofing system and give you an honest assessment. If damage is isolated and the underlying structure is sound, repairs may be sufficient. If there is widespread deterioration, we'll recommend a full replacement that protects your home for decades to come.

faqs.q: How much does a roof replacement cost in Pembroke Park, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Most roof replacements in Pembroke Park range from $10,000 to $22,000 depending on the size, complexity, and product selection. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified homeowners. We work with multiple lenders to accommodate a variety of credit profiles, and our team will walk you through all available financing options during your free, no-obligation estimate. We believe every Pembroke Park homeowner deserves access to a quality, code-compliant roof regardless of their immediate budget.

faqs.q: What permits are required for a new roof in Pembroke Park, and who handles them?
faqs.a: All roofing replacements in Pembroke Park require a permit pulled through the Town of Pembroke Park Building Department. The permit process includes plan review, inspections at key installation milestones, and a final inspection before the permit is closed. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on your behalf — we prepare all documentation, submit Florida Product Approval numbers for the materials being used, coordinate with the building department, and schedule all required inspections. You receive a properly closed permit upon project completion, which is essential for insurance, resale, and legal compliance purposes.

faqs.q: How long does a roof replacement take in Pembroke Park?
faqs.a: Most residential roof replacements in Pembroke Park are completed within one to three days depending on the size of the home, the product selected, and the condition of the existing decking. Tile roofing systems typically require slightly more time than shingle installations due to the additional labor involved in tile laying and attachment. Permitting timelines vary by municipality, but Premier Impact Windows & Roofing submits permits promptly and works efficiently within the Town of Pembroke Park's review schedule. We'll give you a realistic timeline during your estimate and keep you informed throughout every stage of your project.

faqs.q: Will a new roof lower my homeowners insurance premium in Pembroke Park?
faqs.a: Yes, in most cases significantly. A new hurricane-rated roof that qualifies for Florida's wind mitigation credits — including enhanced deck attachment, a secondary water resistance barrier, and proper fastening patterns — can reduce your wind insurance premium by 20% to 45%. For Pembroke Park homeowners paying typical Broward County insurance rates, that can represent hundreds to over a thousand dollars in annual savings. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs all roofing systems to the standards required to maximize these credits. We recommend scheduling a wind mitigation inspection immediately after your permit is closed and submitting the updated report to your insurance carrier at your next renewal.

cta_headline: Ready to Protect Your Pembroke Park Home with a Hurricane-Rated Roof?
cta_body: Contact Premier Impact Windows & Roofing today for a free, no-obligation roofing estimate and let South Florida's Kings of Service show you why hundreds of Broward County homeowners trust us with their most important investment.
